nutshell.headline: 2 new failures, no code changes in this build: a ~60s QuotaGroup timeout (infra-looking) plus a fast-failing Settings test.
nutshell.causeText: This build shipped no source changes and has no suspect commits, so a code regression is not indicated. The QuotaGroup 'change order flow' test ran ~60.1s before failing — a classic socket/read-timeout signature pointing at QA infra — while the Settings 'Set default countries' test failed in 190ms, which looks like an assertion or test-data issue rather than a timeout. Detailed failure messages weren't captured in the evidence, so this is inferred from durations and the absence of changes.
nutshell.investigation: Both new failures are in different areas with no shared signature: QuotaGroup timed out at ~60s (infra) while Settings failed fast in 190ms (test/data). No code changes shipped, so no commit or developer is implicated.
bullets: QuotaGroup · change order flow — ~60s runtime = timeout/infra signature
Settings · Set default countries — 190ms fast fail, likely assertion/test-data
No code changes in this build → no commit blamed

detail.investigation: 1) QuotaGroup::ChangeOrderFlowTestSteps 'change order flow: request, evaluation, block traffic, approve' is the most significant new failure: it ran ~60.1s before failing, the hallmark of a socket/read timeout (slow QA dependency, 502/504) on a multi-step end-to-end flow. 2) Settings::UserSettingsTestSteps 'Set default countries' failed in just 190ms — too fast for a timeout, more consistent with an assertion or test-data problem. 3) The build contained no source changes and no suspect commits, so a code regression is not indicated and no author can be blamed. 4) The rules engine only saw a coarse 'other' cluster with no dominant signature, and no failure messages/stack excerpts were present in the evidence, so classification relies on durations plus change data. Net: isolated, mixed causes (one infra-timeout, one likely test/data), 99.8% pass, nothing pointing at a code change.
